# Infrastructure Design

Generated by `/gaia-infra-design` for the AcmeAPI service.

sidecar_decision: devops-sidecar/infrastructure-decisions.md

## Environments

| Environment | Purpose | Access Policy | Parity |
|-------------|---------|---------------|--------|
| dev | Developer iteration | Engineering team read-write | Minimal |
| staging | Pre-production validation | Eng + QA read-write | High parity with production |
| production | End-user serving | SRE on-call only | Baseline |

Environment parity strategy: staging mirrors production topology and IAM roles.

## Deployment

- Container orchestration: Kubernetes (EKS) with per-service Deployments and HorizontalPodAutoscaler resources.
- Load balancing: AWS ALB terminates TLS at the edge; internal traffic via Istio.
- Auto-scaling: HPA triggers on CPU > 70%; cluster-autoscaler grows nodes.
- Networking: VPC with private subnets across three AZs; security groups; CloudFront CDN fronts static assets.

## IaC

- IaC tool: Terraform 1.6 with the HashiCorp AWS provider.
- Module structure mirrors the service boundary: one module per service.
- Naming conventions: `{service}-{env}-{resource}`.
- Secrets management: AWS Secrets Manager referenced by ARN.

## Observability

- Logging strategy: structured JSON logs shipped via Fluent Bit to CloudWatch Logs with 30-day retention.
- Metrics and dashboards: Prometheus scrapes each service; Grafana dashboards cover RED and SLO burn-down.
- Distributed tracing: OpenTelemetry SDK in every service; correlation IDs across boundaries.
- Alerting and escalation: SLO-based alerts route through PagerDuty to the SRE on-call rotation.
